rulings.Case Title: LONNETTE ROBINSON ET AL VS. LARUE PERKINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Dissolving Partnership And Appointing Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER KERRY BOWSER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Dissolving Partnership And Appointing Receiver is DENIED. The Court does not have jurisdiction pursuant to the arbitration clause in the partnership agreement (Article VI, 6.02). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2023 line 2. rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT JENNIFER CHOI, COREY TEAGUE DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the 14th cause of action for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action and to allege facts showing that the city employees are not subject to immunity. Demurrer to the 6th cause of action is overruled. Defendants claim that this cause of action is moot because the tenants have been evicted on June 30, 2021 a month after this action was filed (May 25, 2021). Defendant fails to identify where in the operative complaint an allegation is made regarding June 30, 2021 eviction. Defendant further claims that complaint fails to explain what wrongful conduct is to be enjoined. A careful reading of the complaint puts the defendants on notice of such wrongful conduct. Demurrer to causes of action 1,5 and 7-13 is overruled. Defendant challenges the veracity of Plaintiff's allegations regarding claims submission and asked the Court to consider evidence presented in a declaration on a demurrer. To the extent defendants seek judicial notice of such declaration, the Court grants judicial notice of the existence of such declaration, but not of its contents. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT LONDON BREED, THE SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION, PAUL MIYAMOTO, TINA TAM, MICHAEL BOROVINA DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR in part, sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint filed by London Breed, Paul Miyamoto and the San Francisco Utilities Commission is OFF CALENDAR. The Court has heard and ruled on the merits of the demurrer brought by these defendants on September 20, 2021. The Court notes that parties failed to provide a proposed Order to the Court for execution after hearing. Therefore, the Court orders that these Defendants, the moving parties on the prior demurrer, must present an order in conformity with the tentative ruling adopted by the Court as its final ruling on September 20, 2021. The Court further notes that The San Francisco Utilities Commission has previously generally appeared in this action. Demurrer by Tina Tam and Michael Borovina is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the 14th cause of action for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action and to allege facts showing that the city employees are not subject to immunity. Demurrer to the 6th cause of action is overruled. Defendants claim that this cause of action is moot because the tenants have been evicted on June 30, 2021 a month after this action was filed (May 25, 2021). Defendant fails to identify where in the operative complaint an allegation is made regarding June 30, 2021 eviction. Defendant further claims that complaint fails to explain what wrongful conduct is to be enjoined. A careful reading of the complaint puts the defendants on notice of such wrongful conduct. Demurrer to causes of action 1,5 and 7-13 is overruled. Defendant challenges the veracity of Plaintiff's allegations regarding claims submission and asked the Court to consider evidence presented in a declaration on a demurrer. To the extent defendants seek judicial notice of such declaration, the Court grants judicial notice of the existence of such declaration, but not of its contents. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ryan James Pattersons Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT RYAN PATTERSON Defendant Ryan James Pattersons Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike is GRANTED. The motion is timely filed and properly served by mail to Plaintiff's mailing address of record (See Proof of Service filed on March 13, 2023). Moving party sustained its burden under the first prong of Anti-SLAPP. Plaintiff failed to carry her burden under the second prong of the Anti-Slapp to show a probability of prevailing on the merits. The Court notes that Plaintiff appears to misread Patterson declaration. Mr. Patterson declares that he is a shareholder of Zacks, Freedman and Patterson, not a shareholder of Crashpadz. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: UNITED BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. KINNEY HOTEL, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Instructing And Authorizing Receiver To List And Market 410 Eddy Street, San Francisco, California For Sale; And Confirming Sale Procedures
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2023 line 8. PLAINTIFF UNITED BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Instructing And Authorizing Receiver To List And Market 410 Eddy Street, San Francisco, California For Sale; And Confirming Sale Procedures is DENIED. Defendants defaulted on December 21, 2022. Plaintiff could have obtained a judgment in this action in department 514 at any time thereafter. There is no indication in the register of actions that Plaintiff attempted to or scheduled a hearing to obtain a judgment even though several Case Management Orders were issued by department 610 for Plaintiff to obtain a default judgment by the time of the CMC. No authority is provided for the proposition that the requested relief is necessary and proper in light of the procedural posture of this case. Plaintiff shall proceed with default judgment forthwith to address the pending CMC order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2023 line 9. DEFENDANT LAURA DECARAVA DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to serve a Warning Notice per SFRO 37.9(o) for the violation upon which the operative Notice is based. SFRO 37.9(o) requires the violation be described and tenant be provided with an opportunity to correct "such violation." Violation described in the attached Warning Notice and the act required to correct it, is different from the operative notice of termination of tenancy. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
